rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Sandra Gustafson (Formerly Known As Sandra Demartini) To Provide Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 2); And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set 2); And Request For Sanctions Against Said Defendant And Her Counsel, Jointly And Severally
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, July 23, 2015, Line 1, PLAINTIFFS VINFORMATION INC, AND MOUNTAIN VIEW VINTNERS INC'S Motion To Compel Defendant Sandra Gustafson (Formerly Known As Sandra Demartini) To Provide Responses To Special Interrogatories (Set 2); And Further Responses To Request For Production Of Documents (Set 2); And Request For Sanctions Against Said Defendant And Her Counsel, Jointly And Severally. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: GRANTED in part and DENIED in part, as follows. Plaintiffs' Objections to Evidence Submitted by Defendant in Opposition to Plaintiffs' Discovery Motion are OVERRULED. Plaintiffs fail to cite the exact language of the declaration to which they raise an objection. See Schoendorf v. U.D. Registry, Inc. (2002) 97 Cal. App. 4th 227, 240; See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2015) 9:102.5(1). Furthermore, the objection are moot in light of the ruling on the sanctions. The Motion is GRANTED as to all of Special Interrogatories (Set 2); and Request for Production of Documents (Set 2), excluding #15. Defendant Sandra Gustafson shall provide code-compliant, verified responses to each of these requests within two weeks of notice of entry of order. Plaintiffs' request for sanctions is DENIED. If a request for sanctions is included in a motion to compel, the notice of motion must contain a request for sanctions and must: (1) Name all parties and attorneys against whom sanctions are being sought; (2) Specify the type of sanction sought; (3) Cite the authority for such sanctions. CCP § 2023.040; See Weil & Brown, Cal. Practice Guide: Civil Procedure Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2015) 8:1194. If neither party contests this tentative ruling, plaintiffs shall email a proposed order to pcc@manningllp.com by 5pm today. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CENTRAL GARDENS, INC. VS. ROBIN DEWAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 23, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT ROBIN DEWAR, DENNIS O'LEARY'S Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint. The motion is granted. There is an extremely liberal policy in allowing the filing of a compulsory cross-complaint and plaintiff fails to demonstrate substantial evidence of bad faith. See Foot's Transfer & Storage Co. v. Sup. Crt. (1980) 114 Cal.App.3d 897, 902. Either party may seek a trial continuance before the Presiding Judge in department 206. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)

rulings.Case Title: FADI HANANI VS. MICHAIL A HATTER ET AL****TRANSFERRED TO LOS ANGELES COUNTY****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Change Of Venue
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, July 23, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT MICHAIL HATTER'S Motion For Change Of Venue. Granted. The matter is transferred to Los Angeles County. Defendant has established that he resides in Los Angeles County and none of the statutory exceptions to the general venue rule apply. (See CCP sec. 395(a).) The contract does not specify where it is to be performed. (Complaint, Ex. A, Partnership Agreement.) Defendant's declaration establishes that he entered into the contract at his home in Los Angeles County. (Declaration of Michail Hatter, par. 5.) Plaintiff states in his declaration that the contract was actually accepted at Plaintiff's residence, but his declaration is silent on where his residence is located, and is therefore insufficient to refute Defendant's declaration. (Declaration of Fadi Hanani, par. 2.) The plain language of the Partnership Agreement limits the selection of San Francisco to arbitration, and therefore is irrelevant to determining venue for a court action. (Complaint, Ex. A, Partnership Agreement, par. 19.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/SB)
